intro.title: Hi, I'm Tony Yuan
intro.desc:
MS ECE @ UCLA • Electrical Engineering
Focus: Mechanical design, Analog IC design and robotic systems integration.
Email: zehaoyuan2002@gmail.com

items.title: RoboMaster 2024 Hero Robot
items.desc: High-precision launch system, capable of launching 42 mm golf balls to a 20 × 20 cm target at 25 m with a firing rate of 2 Hz. Features an elevating sighting module and integrated laser rangefinder for real-time ballistic computation and compensation.

items.title: RoboMaster 2026 Drone
items.desc: Designed a center-plate-free RoboMaster drone, delivering full 750-round payload at only 25% size and 75% weight versus the 2025 university platform average.

items.title: RoboMaster 2022 Swerve Infantry Robot
items.desc: Designed and built a swerve-drive infantry robot with a full carbon-fiber structure and in-wheel motors, weighing only 17 kg including battery and referee system.

items.title: Wheelleg Balance Robot
items.desc: UIUC ECE Milestone Best Overall Project. Capable of carrying over 3 kg of payload, with STM32F1 and BMI088 IMU, and an integrated DC-DC power system with full circuit protection.

items.title: Ongoing Projects
items.desc: Projects currently in progress or under development.

items.title: Project Gallery
items.desc: A collection of project images and highlights.

event1.title: M.S. Electrical Engineering · UCLA
event1.desc: Graduate study in Analog / Mixed-Signal IC and Power Electronics. Focus areas include CMOS PLL design and tape-out, ADC/DAC architecture, multi-phase GaN power converters, and robotics / mechatronics system integration.

event2.title: Hardware Engineer Intern · MATRIX Robotics
event2.desc: Worked on mechanical design and prototyping for robotic systems, supporting mechanism development, system integration, and iterative hardware improvement.

event3.title: RoboMaster Team Captain · iRM-Illini Robotics
event3.desc: Led the RoboMaster competition team and coordinated mechanical design, manufacturing, and integration for multiple competition robots. Managed cross-team collaboration and system integration.

event4.title: Mechanical Team Lead · RoboMaster
event4.desc: Oversaw robot mechanical architecture, fabrication workflows, and subsystem integration. Responsible for manufacturability and rapid iteration during competition build cycles.

event5.title: Robotics System Development
event5.desc: Collaborated with ZJUI RoboMaster team. Designed Drone and Sentry Robot mechanical systems and supported system integration.

event6.title: B.S. Electrical Engineering · University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign
event6.desc: Undergraduate study focused on Mechanical, power electronics, and robotics engineering.

event7.title: FRC Robotics · Iron Maple 5516
event7.desc: Hands-on mechanical design and build experience for FIRST Robotics Competition robots. Early exposure to robotics engineering and team leadership.
